string 2: Kuga!
Over here! Give us the data!
Ngh... Never... I'll never give it up!

You mean to say that is where the
research data on Parallelmon is stored?
You must give that to us! Now!
Data on Parallelmon?

That is just what we have been looking for.
Hand that over, nice and easy.

If we can make use of Parallelmon's ability to traverse
space-time, we can isolate all lesser Digimon.

Silence! You will do no such thing!
There are no lesser Digimon. Our lives are all equal!

Oh? Then how do you propose to resolve
the current situation we are in?

By traveling through space-time, we can uncover the origins 
of the X Program. That may allow us to find a way forward.

We do not have that kind of time! What will it matter if we 
find a solution after the world has already been destroyed?!

Enough! You are all making the same fundamental mistake!
Parallelmon's data is not ours to begin with!

These people risked their lives to obtain it!
Yet now you propose to take it by force? Words fail me!
Ngh...!
Kuga, wait!
Damn it!
You think... one of these leads to where they went?
We shall simply have to press on and hope! Hurry!
"...Kuga! Calm yourself. The settings are incorrect!"
"I-I'm sorry, Dr. Simmons!"
What's this we're seeing?
A memory of Kuga's?
"I hate to admit it, but... Simmons is the real deal."

"I just don't get it! Why should SHE have so much talent?!
A woman who became a scientist because of some anime?!"

"My love of science is so much greater than hers!
I'd give my life if it meant contributing to scientific progress!"

"What you've created in my absence is
nothing but a weapon of mass murder."

"I'm sure even you have come to regret that.
After all, you can hardly ignore all the destruction outside."
"Urgh..."

"Come work as my assistant again.
That's all I have to say to you."
"Wait...! A-Are you really going to let me off so easily?!"

"I'm the reason everything's turned out like this!
It's all my fault!"
"Don't flatter yourself, Kuga."

"If your work alone was what made the world turn out
like this... well, then, that would be great news."

"That would mean with my work alone,
I could restore it to what it was before."

"But right now, I need every helping hand I can get.
You've got hands, don't you? I'm sure you'll be able to help."

"Come on. The weight of the sins you bear isn't so great.
So loosen up, and let's get to work!"
"Kuga, don't be an idiot!"
"What good will it do for you to steal that data?!"

"You're asking ME that?!
No, I'm the one with questions for you, Simmons!"

"How is it you refuse to act, when
the answer is staring you in the face?"

"Parallelmon has the ability to travel freely
through space-time, just as you predicted!"

"If we can harness that power, we may be able to
alter this apocalyptic situation we're living in!"

"We've already tried and failed to harness
the power of Digimon. Have you forgotten?"

"That failure was just a stepping stone for this to succeed!
Everything's been guiding us toward this one answer!"
"...You truly are a hopeless fool."
"Oh, why...? Why are you always like this?!"

"Why is it that you have so much talent...
yet refuse to use it for what's right?!"

"Wasn't it the unquestioned assumption that eliminating all
Digimon was right that caused D-SAT to go off the deep end?"

"No! D-SAT failed because... because I lacked the
talent and ability to build that weapon properly!"

"Yes... Science can solve any problem, provided we have the 
skill to implement it. That's what has brought us this far!"

"Heaven's mistake... was in blessing you with such genius
when you do not love science! If only you would get serious...!"

"If I had your gifts, I'd use them properly! And as a man utterly
devoted to science, I'm the one who deserves them!"

I'll never hand over this data! I'm the only one
who can make proper use of all that talent!

The advancement of science comes before all! Only by staying
true to this principle will we solve humanity's problems!

I am afraid we have our own principles that we must hold to.
Prepare for our worst.

Those "principles" you speak of are arbitrary at best!
Don't you see how wrong this all is?

It is not we who determine right or wrong!
It is divine will!

They truly mean to take that data by force...
That man is in danger. We must help!
